issues.title: Formalize Open Quantum Problem #24: Secret key from all entangled states

divergence_notes: Count vocabulary: draft uses Mathlib's SimpleGraph.copyCount G (cycleGraph 5) (number of subgraphs of G isomorphic to C5); both hosted proofs define numC5 G = #{labelled C5 maps Fin 5 -> V} / 10 (labelled embeddings divided by |D5| = 10). The two counts are equal on every graph (each C5-subgraph carries exactly 10 labellings), so this is neither a strengthening nor a weakening.

Form: draft wraps the statement as answer(True) <-> forall n G, ... (FC house style for solved yes/no questions); hosted are bare theorems parametrised by (n : Nat) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin (5 * n))) (hG : G.CliqueFree 3). Same content.
Triangle-free rendered as G.CliqueFree 3 and vertex type Fin (5 * n) in draft and in both hosted final theorems -- no divergence on either.

Hosted-vs-hosted: final statements agree modulo naming (plby erdos_pentagon_conjecture vs jayyhk erdos_24) and namespacing of numC5. No disagreement. Note: the inputs.md extracts showed only intermediate flag-algebra lemmas (flag_bound_all_graphs / flag_bound_with_c5); the final theorems were read from the full hosted files.
ams_rationale: AMS 5 (combinatorics): extremal graph theory, counting C5 subgraphs in triangle-free graphs; matches FC graph problems 61/85.
